channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Elliott Management and the art of telling bosses they’re wrong
item.description: The hedge fund has industrialised shareholder activism

item.title: The Anglosphere is increasingly miserable
item.description: The World Happiness Report shows it diverging from the rest of the world

item.title: Nigeria’s President Begins a Historic U.K. Visit
item.description: Despite their violent colonial past, the two nations have grown much closer in recent years.

item.title: U.S. Lawmakers Grill Trump’s Intel Chief on Iran Nuclear Threat
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard faced bipartisan scrutiny over the administration’s justifications for the Iran war.

item.title: The Iran war could sap American military power for years
item.description: It is devouring munitions and exhausting an already stretched navy

item.title: Can Pakistan and Afghanistan De-Escalate?
item.description: The strike on a Kabul hospital was the deadliest single incident in the conflict so far.

item.title: What if Donald Trump decided to ban oil exports?
item.description: Trying to keep prices low that way could backfire spectacularly

item.title: Tehran Vows to Strike Gulf Oil, Gas Facilities
item.description: The threat is in retaliation for alleged Israeli strikes on Iran’s South Pars gas field.

item.title: How Ukraine and Europe got caught in a geopolitical lovers’ tiff
item.description: A testing moment for steadfast allies

item.title: Xi Just Can’t Shake GDP Worship
item.description: Chinese officials are being asked to do everything all at once.

item.title: Japan Should Help Sink China’s Invasion Fleet
item.description: Sanae Takaichi committing to Taiwan’s defense would help keep the peace.

item.title: How the Iran war is weakening Donald Trump
item.description: An unpopular conflict and costly fuel could hobble his presidency

item.title: Iran Isn’t Trump’s Only War
item.description: Even as Trump wages war on Tehran, he’s reigniting his economic war against U.S. trading partners.

item.title: How Zara fought off H&M and Shein
item.description: A touch of luxury has helped

item.title: Is cheap energy the key to China gaining AI supremacy?
item.description: It could be its biggest advantage over the West

item.title: The Pros and Cons of Negotiating in Public
item.description: When Trump’s unconventional approach to diplomacy does—and doesn’t—work.

item.title: BRICS Is Divided on Iran. So Are NATO and the G-7.
item.description: The bloc isn’t a geopolitical alliance, and it shouldn’t be expected to function like one.

item.title: India’s Iran Blind Spot and the Gulf War: A Litmus Test of Strategic Autonomy
item.description: New Delhi probably hoped that the conflict would end quickly and hence it could pursue a hands-off policy. That hasn’t worked out.

item.title: The next phase of artificial intelligence may require very different processors
item.description: The GPUs that powered the AI boom can’t handle the workload

item.title: China is a serious contender in the race for fusion energy
item.description: Could it be the first to build a commercial reactor?

item.title: Top AI models underperform in languages other than English
item.description: Those turning to them for health advice are most at risk

item.title: Corruption Allegations at Kyrgyzneftgas Put Tashiev’s Network in the Crosshairs
item.description: The head of Kyrgyzstan’s State Tax Service has alleged that “Kyrgyzneftegaz was completely under the control of the State Committee for National Security,” amid an unfolding corruption case.

item.title: Africa Isn’t the World’s “Climate Solution”
item.description: Africa is increasingly portrayed as a global leader on climate solutions. But this narrative presents climate justice as a technical financing challenge, rather than a matter of holding historic emitters accountable, thereby reinforcing structural arrangements that sustain the continent’s continued exploitation.

item.title: A dirty deal with Cuba would be better than the alternatives
item.description: A prolonged blockade risks creating a humanitarian crisis on America’s doorstep

item.title: Human Rights Due Diligence May Repeat the Limitations of CSR
item.description: Emerging frameworks still rely heavily on corporate disclosure and monitoring systems – like CSR initiatives, which often fail to make fundamental improvements in workers’ lives.

item.title: Nvidia Export Curbs Cloud South Korea’s Chip Outlook, Fueling China’s AI Ambitions
item.description: Seoul’s semiconductor giants face collateral damage as Washington’s tech blockade inadvertently accelerates Beijing’s domestic hardware breakthroughs.

item.title: America’s Permanent Tariff Uncertainty Will Drive Up Costs
item.description: The US Supreme Court’s rejection of the Trump administration’s legal justification for tariffs, and the administration's quest for a new one, has created a patchwork of temporary measures, sectoral probes, bilateral bargaining, and national-security exceptions. There will now be three additional costs associated with US trade policy.

item.title: How to Fix Democracy? Out With the Politicians!
item.description: Democracy scholar Hélène Landemore has a radical new proposal.

item.title: Responding to the Israel-US vs Iran War: Indonesia’s Board of Peace Dilemma Intensifies
item.description: The Israel-U.S. vs Iran war is forcing Indonesia to reassess its Gaza Board of Peace stance, balancing neutrality, public pressure, and support for Palestine.

item.title: The India-US Interim Trade Deal and New Tariffs
item.description: Insights from Pratik Dattani.

item.title: The World’s Tax-the-Rich Debate Is Heating Up
item.description: From millionaire activists to G-20 proposals, efforts to combat wealth inequality are advancing across the United States and Europe.

item.title: Oil Shocks Are Rewriting Southeast Asia’s Auto Market for Legacy Carmakers
item.description: If established brands do not pivot to electric vehicles immediately, they risk losing market share to a new generation of competitors across the region.

item.title: Kazakhstan and the Board of Peace: Humanitarian Aid and Middle Power Ambitions
item.description: Kazakhstan’s commitments at the Board of Peace are backed by a multi-layered, field-tested approach to international development assistance.

item.title: Fear and Loathing in Pyongyang: What the Iran Strikes Imply for North Korea
item.description: Alarmed by the example of Iran, Pyongyang will learn its lessons – and learn them fast.

item.title: Reimagining Gulf Security
item.description: The Iran war has exposed the limits of the “protection for sale” system that has defined Gulf geopolitics for decades. Instead of continuing to rely on US military guarantees, which will always remain conditional and incomplete, Gulf countries should pursue a shift toward security rooted in regional economic integration.

item.title: The Kabul Hospital Strike and the Escalation Logic Driving Afghanistan-Pakistan Conflict
item.description: The destruction of a rehabilitation hospital is difficult to reconcile with a strategy aimed at neutralizing militants.

item.title: The Japan-US Summit: Takaichi’s Impossible Task
item.description: Japan was prepared for a successful summit. Then the U.S. bombed Iran, and Trump demanded Japan send ships to the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: China’s Censorship Is the Most Pressing Threat to Freedom of Expression
item.description: Advanced technologies are propelling the CCP’s capabilities for suppressing speech to an entirely new level, both within and beyond China’s borders.

item.title: The Populist International Is Falling Apart
item.description: Trump’s war in Iran is blowing up MAGA’s alliance with Europe’s far right.

item.title: Middle East Dispatch newsletter: Iran’s mood shifts
item.description: The immediate imperative is no longer political change but personal safety, writes Nick Pelham, our Middle East correspondent

item.title: A spy scandal upends Slovenia’s election campaign
item.description: Embarrassing videos and a mysterious Israeli intelligence firm

item.title: American Hegemony Is Collapsing Before Our Eyes
item.description: The global security network that long distinguished US power took generations to build, but it is now unraveling under Donald Trump's watch. With traditional partners opting out of the Iran war, we could well be seeing the end of the global primacy that most Americans alive today have always taken for granted.

item.title: Can Companies Buy Their Way Into the S&P 500?
item.description: Joining the S&P 500 can transform a company’s fortunes, making confidence in the selection process essential for investors and markets alike. But a new study finds that companies purchasing S&P credit ratings were more likely to be admitted, raising questions about the influence of commercial incentives on firms' inclusion.

item.title: The Lasting Wounds of the War in Ukraine
item.description: Both Russia and Ukraine will struggle to reintegrate millions of veterans.

item.title: How to Raise the Odds of Regime Change in Iran
item.description: America can make it easier for Iranians to revolt.

item.title: The Gulf Goes Backward
item.description: The Iran war is returning the Gulf to a more insular, conflict-prone era.

item.title: Trump Endangers the Handover of the UK’s ‘Last Colony’
item.description: Trump has changed his tune on the deal to hand over the Chagos Islands to Mauritius, citing concerns over the fate of the U.S. military base at Diego Garcia.
